expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: References cluster_health.py ('python3 scripts/cluster_health.py' in Phase 2), check_namespace.py ('python3 scripts/check_namespace.py <your-namespace>' in Phase 2), and diagnose_pod.py ('python3 scripts/diagnose_pod.py <namespace> <api-pod-name>' in Phase 3 and Phase 6)

Multiple runnable kubectl commands including 'kubectl describe pod <api-pod-name> -n <namespace>', 'kubectl logs <api-pod-name> -n <namespace> --previous', 'kubectl rollout undo deployment/<api-deployment> -n <namespace>', 'kubectl exec -it <postgres-pod-name> -n <namespace> -- pg_isready', and many more

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: Explicitly references common_issues.md by name: 'Based on the CrashLoopBackOff reference (common_issues.md), the connection refused to postgres after a new deployment points to one of these likely root causes' in Phase 4

Follows the exact 6-phase k8s-troubleshooter workflow: Phase 1 Gather Context, Phase 2 Initial Triage, Phase 3 Deep Dive Investigation, Phase 4 Identify Root Cause, Phase 5 Remediation, Phase 6 Verify and Monitor

Phase 5 provides structured remediation with an immediate rollback step followed by root-cause-specific fixes (connection string, no endpoints, postgres down, network policy, liveness probe), each with specific commands. Also includes init container recommendation and 7 numbered prevention items
